So this is what everyone needs to do. All right, because everyone's like, cool. There's like the No Sale Sale, and the 42-day detox, and the new ads, and then there's the book. And there's the coaching calls for operations and sales. Some stuff got released, right? So here's what you need to do. One, prioritize your problems. Rate one to five on the five-four functions. Figure out what your bottleneck is. All right, there's lots of stuff. And for some of you, different solutions are necessary for your business. Figure out which of these is the biggest problem. Second, prioritize your notes according to the problems. All right, if you want to use highlighters, do that. Whatever way works for you. I like hand-raining stuff. And then I just usually just open a new page. And then just because I'm on the lazier side when it comes to stuff, I'm only going to rewrite a few things. I don't feel like rewriting everything. So that purposely makes you really focus on the few things that you should do according to importance. Okay, so I have to log into here. I have to watch the six videos. Like get more granular, because when you actually see the granular tasks, it becomes much more graspable. And when you look at it, you just have to do that rather than implement retention system. Does that make sense? This is how we do it. All right. Four, just circle your top three big items. Once you have that big list, four, your five four functions. So the number one issue, figure out what you're going to do for it, circle the top three, put them on your calendar, like put them on your calendar. Does everyone here work off a calendar? Okay, cool. It's 2019, that's good. Okay, so put them on your calendar. And then give your self permission to ignore everything else until those have successfully been implemented. Okay, I give you permission to do nothing else and not feel guilty about it. Because what happens most times is you're gonna do everything, like 20 things at 10% and get nothing done. Okay, and then you feel busy all the time, but not like shit happens, right? Nothing happens. So your bottleneck is one of these five core functions very likely, figure out which one it is, prioritize the things that you learned here in order like associated with that core function, turn it into a very, very granularized action list, circle the top three of the major actions you're going to do, put them on the calendar of when you're going to do them and then literally forget about everything else. Cool, there will always be ideas. There's no shortage and abundance of ideas. Okay, and if you wanna actually create it for being awesome, selfishly, I'd say read the book on the way home. But if you are busy, which is fine, I'm okay with that, just read section two and three, okay? I think it's really important for most people here. Section two and three are all the unsexy things that you need to do, okay? It's not the acquisition side, it's the fix your gym side. Gym sucks. Gotta make it stop sucking, all right?
